value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Foreigners staying in Japan can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures and tax-free purchases during their stay.

All foreign nationals/people who need to obtain a short-term visa to Japan and currently reside in Australia, Brazil, Cambodia, Canada, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Taiwan, United Kingdom, and U.S.A. are eligible to apply for a tourist visa online.
The average cost of a ryokan stay is between 15,000 and 30,000 yen per person and night
Tourists can bring in goods for personal use purchased abroad whose total market value is less than 200,000 Yen duty-free.
From 2025, Japan will require all incoming tourists to have private medical insurance to cover potential medical costs during their stay.
Starting March 1, 2026, Himeji Castle admission price for non-residents more than doubled from 1,000 yen to 2,500 yen
Osaka Castle admission costs 600 yen
Junguria Okinawa theme park will charge different entry prices for foreigners and Japanese residents starting July 2025
Most IC cards require a security deposit of 500 yen when purchasing the card.
The Japan Rail Pass is available for non-Japanese nationals visiting Japan on a tourist visa (Temporary Visitor status).
If you are purchasing a pass to register with your Welcome Suica card, you must pay for the pass at the same time.
A full meal in tourist-populated areas in Japan can be found for approximately 10 CAD, including tax, covering fast food like ramen or hamburgers and teishoku set meals.
Near Kyoto Station or downtown Kyoto, including areas near Kyoto-Kawaramachi Station, Pontocho Alley, and Shichijō Station in southern Higashiyama

Ryokan are traditional Japanese inns that offer a unique opportunity to experience traditional Japanese lifestyle and hospitality, including tatami floors, futon beds, Japanese style baths, and local cuisine.
Visiting interior of Phoenix Hall (Hou-ou-do) is open from 9:30 to 16:10, with tours every 20 minutes and a maximum of 50 people per visit
The Tokaido Shinkansen connects Tokyo and Osaka in about four hours.
Using an IC card such as Suica or PASMO makes transfers between JR, private rail, and metro lines seamless, saving time and avoiding multiple tickets.
Japan is an island country in East Asia, located in the Pacific Ocean, divided into 9 regions: Hokkaido, Tohoku, Kanto, Chubu, Kansai (Kinki), Chugoku, Shikoku, Kyushu, and Okinawa.
Many ryokans require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room to maintain hygiene and respect traditional Japanese culture.
Hearton Hotel Kyoto is located in Kyoto
The average food cost in Japan is $30–50 per day for most travelers in 2026; budget travelers can eat well for $15–25 per day using convenience stores and chain restaurants.
In Japanese dining etiquette, it is considered impolite to raise food above mouth level while eating.
Just a 6-minute walk from Shinjuku Station
Japan eSIMs and prepaid SIM cards can be purchased online before travel, with providers like Journey Japan eSIM offering affordable and reliable plans.

Wireless hotspots are widely available in major Japanese cities, but for guaranteed access in remote areas, renting Wi-Fi is recommended. Major international airports in Japan provide free Wi-Fi within their terminals, with connection methods varying by airport.
Many convenience stores such as 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, and Lawson, as well as some chain restaurants and discount stores, operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.

eSIM Japan plans start from $0.6 per GB

Residents with bank accounts in Japan can apply for a debit card, which may require selecting payment options for online shopping when setting up the card. Seven Bank provides ATMs in 7-Eleven stores, and PayPay is a mobile payment service for cashless smartphone transactions. Foreigners often use English-friendly services like Revolut and Wise, which offer debit cards usable in Japan. Visa and Mastercard cards are widely accepted for purchases, payments, and ATM withdrawals.
Major international credit card brands such as Visa, Mastercard, and JCB are widely accepted in Japan at various establishments including shops, restaurants, hotels, and transportation services.
Booking a connectivity solution with NINJA WiFi before arrival allows travelers to move directly from customs to transportation without delays and ensures dependable internet access throughout Japan.
A deposit is required to buy an IC travel card in Japan
When traveling in Japan, it is recommended to carry cash as a backup because some locations may experience payment errors with credit cards.
The most commonly accepted contactless payment smartphone apps or QR code payment methods in Japan are PayPay, LinePay, Rpay (Rakuten Pay), and MerPay.

Use the Japan Rail Pass to travel efficiently on Shinkansen bullet trains and other rail lines, and consider using RailNinja to compare train types, fares, and make Green Car reservations with specific seat selections.

Japan Rail Pass holders can use Shinkansen bullet trains without extra payments during the validity of their pass, and can access all Japan Railways Group (JR) trains, buses, and ferry services with 7, 14, or 21-day passes by showing their pass at ticket offices and entering platforms through special gates.
When eating noodle dishes in Japan, local culture dictates that you slurp rather than chew, which some say enhances the flavor.
Situated between the prefecture’s dual peninsulas, the capital city of Aomori in northern Japan
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen travel time is just over two hours
House and apartment rentals are available all over Japan
To avoid crowds at major sights in Japan, use your jet lag to your advantage by going as early as possible in the day.
Many hotels in Japan, especially ryokans, require guests to remove their shoes at the entrance or in their room.

Ryokans often provide rooms with private open-air baths, but most ryokans also have rooms without private open-air baths. If you want a room with a private open-air bath, you must select that type of room when making a reservation.
The Japan Rail Pass is a great travel companion for long-distance journeys across Japan, allowing access to Shinkansen bullet trains, Green Car reservations, and specific seat selections.

Capsule hotels in Japan typically have very limited space for suitcases, often requiring guests to keep luggage on the bed or blocking exits; bathrooms are usually all-in-one plastic units with walls, bathtub, floor, and basin made of the same material and color; beds are semi-double size regardless of booking for one or two people; rooms include a desk with a TV, a tiny fridge underneath, a kettle with mugs and tea, a full-length mirror, and some hanging space near the exit.

Restaurants specializing in various Japanese foods such as Sushi-ya, Soba-ya, Udon-ya, Ramen-ya, Kare-ya, Gyudon-ya, Tempura-ya, Family restaurants, Chinese, Italian, French, and Indian restaurants are found all across Japan.
Many restaurants tend to close for a few hours after lunch time around 2:00 p.m. and reopen around 6:00 p.m. for dinner
Western-style hotels in Japan are usually located close to train stations and offer clean and comfortable lodgings.

Typical dinner time in Japan generally falls between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m.
Japanese cuisine emphasizes balance and harmony, focusing on creating an aesthetically pleasing and holistic dining experience that respects natural flavors and quality ingredients.
Saying “itadakimasu” before a meal is a way of expressing gratitude in Japanese dining etiquette.

IC travel cards can be used to make payments at many convenience stores, shops, and vending machines in Japan. Physical IC travel cards can be purchased at station ticket counters or automatic ticket vending machines at most railway stations. A deposit is required to buy an IC travel card.
Travelers should plan ahead and use pocket Wi-Fi, a SIM card, or eSIM during their travels in Japan because free Wi-Fi is limited and mobile roaming tends to be expensive.

Japan's major international airports provide free Wi-Fi within their terminals, allowing travelers to connect as soon as they enter the airport buildings.
